events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need parse scenario. Field service, electrical, receptacles & switches, observed condition: open ground. Work stage rough-complete. Component condition installed-defective. Need identify component/condition and visible defect cues. Decision fail because open ground is non-compliant and shock hazard. Risk high/critical? Open ground on receptacle means no equipment grounding conductor connection, shock hazard, especially if device is grounded type (3-prong) used with equipment grounding required. NEC 406.4 requires receptacles with grounding contacts be grounded; 210.8 GFCI maybe. 406.9 maybe installation. Risk high. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Findings: open ground at receptacle/switch, missing/broken ground connection, energized device without EGC. Actions: verify EGC continuity, terminate ground wire to receptacle ground terminal/box ground, replace if damaged, retest. percent_complete maybe 80? rough-complete. value not asked 0. confidence 0.95. event_time 0. order empty. workable empty. references NEC 406.4(D), 210.8 maybe. Need ensure JSON valid.

    "Open ground at receptacle/switch: equipment grounding conductor is not connected or continuity is lost",
    "Visible defect cues: missing or loose green/bare ground wire at device ground terminal, ground terminal screw absent, or ground conductor not bonded to grounded metal box/yoke",
    "Three-prong grounding-type receptacle installed without an effective ground path",
    "Shock hazard: exposed conductive parts can become energized under fault conditions"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "De-energize the circuit before servicing",
    "Verify continuity of the equipment grounding conductor back to the panel",
    "Terminate the ground wire to the receptacle/switch ground terminal and bond to the grounded metal box if applicable",
    "Replace the device if the ground terminal is damaged",
    "Re-energize and test for correct wiring and ground integrity"
  ],
  "rationale": "An open ground on a receptacle or switch leaves the device without an effective fault-current path. NEC 406.4 requires grounding-type receptacles to be grounded, and 406.9 addresses proper installation. Because the installation is defective and creates a shock hazard, the work fails inspection at the rough-complete stage.",
  "references": ["NEC 406.4", "NEC 406.9", "NEC 210.8"]
}
